AL.notes: Alabama requires probate for most estates. Small estates under $25,000 may use a simplified affidavit procedure.

AK.notes: Alaska has adopted the Uniform Probate Code, providing a streamlined process.

AZ.notes: Arizona allows small estate affidavits for estates under $75,000 in personal property.

AR.notes: Small estates under $100,000 may use a summary administration.

CA.notes: California has one of the highest probate thresholds. Estates under $184,500 (adjusted periodically) can use a simplified affidavit. California probate fees are set by statute and can be substantial.

CO.notes: Colorado has adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Small estates under $80,000 may use an affidavit.

CT.notes: Connecticut has an estate tax with a $12.92 million threshold for 2024. Small estates under $40,000 have simplified procedures.

DE.notes: Delaware eliminated its estate tax in 2018. Small estates under $30,000 may use simplified procedures.

DC.notes: DC has an estate tax with a $4 million threshold. Small estates under $40,000 have simplified procedures.

FL.notes: Florida has no estate tax. Formal probate is often required. Summary administration available for estates under $75,000 or when deceased has been dead more than 2 years.

GA.notes: Georgia has a 60-day creditor claim period from date of notice publication.

HI.notes: Hawaii has an estate tax with a $5.49 million threshold. Small estates under $100,000 may use simplified procedures.

ID.notes: Idaho has adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Small estates under $100,000 may use simplified procedures.

IL.notes: Illinois has an estate tax with a $4 million threshold. Small estates under $100,000 in personal property may use simplified procedures.

IN.notes: Indiana eliminated its inheritance tax in 2013. Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures.

IA.notes: Iowa is phasing out its inheritance tax by 2025.

KS.notes: Small estates under $40,000 may use simplified procedures in Kansas.

KY.notes: Kentucky has an inheritance tax (not estate tax) for non-spouse, non-lineal heirs.

LA.notes: Louisiana uses a unique civil law system. Succession (probate) procedures differ significantly from other states. Small successions under $125,000 have simplified procedures.

ME.notes: Maine has an estate tax with a $6.41 million threshold for 2024. Small estates under $40,000 may use simplified procedures.

MD.notes: Maryland has both an estate tax (threshold $5 million) and an inheritance tax for certain heirs. Small estates under $50,000 use a simplified procedure.

MA.notes: Massachusetts has an estate tax with a $2 million threshold — one of the lowest in the nation. All estates over $2M owe tax on the full amount. Small estates under $25,000 use simplified procedures.

MI.notes: Michigan has no estate tax. Small estates under $15,000 may use a simplified affidavit.

MN.notes: Minnesota has an estate tax with a $3 million threshold. Small estates under $75,000 may use simplified procedures.

MS.notes: Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures in Mississippi.

MO.notes: Missouri has no estate tax. Estates under $40,000 use a simplified refusal of letters procedure.

MT.notes: Montana has adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures.

NE.notes: Nebraska has an inheritance tax (not estate tax). Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures.

NV.notes: Nevada has no estate or inheritance tax. Small estates under $25,000 in personal property may use simplified affidavit procedures.

NH.notes: New Hampshire has no estate tax. Small estates under $10,000 in personal property have simplified procedures.

NJ.notes: New Jersey eliminated its estate tax in 2018 but still has an inheritance tax for non-lineal heirs. Small estates under $20,000 may use simplified procedures.

NM.notes: New Mexico has no estate tax. Small estates under $50,000 in personal property may use simplified procedures.

NY.notes: New York has an estate tax with a $6.94 million threshold for 2024. New York has a 'cliff' — if the estate exceeds 105% of the threshold, the entire estate is taxed. Small estates under $50,000 use a voluntary administration proceeding.

NC.notes: North Carolina has no estate tax. Small estates under $20,000 in personal property may use simplified affidavit procedures.

ND.notes: North Dakota has adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures.

OH.notes: Ohio eliminated its estate tax in 2013. Small estates under $35,000 may use a summary release procedure.

OK.notes: Oklahoma has no estate tax. Small estates under $20,000 may use simplified affidavit procedures.

OR.notes: Oregon has an estate tax with a $1 million threshold — the lowest in the nation. All estates over $1M owe Oregon estate tax. Small estates under $275,000 in total assets may use simplified procedures.

PA.notes: Pennsylvania has an inheritance tax (not estate tax) for all heirs except spouses. Small estates under $50,000 may use a simplified procedure.

RI.notes: Rhode Island has an estate tax with a $1.73 million threshold. Small estates under $15,000 may use simplified procedures.

SC.notes: South Carolina has no estate tax. Small estates under $25,000 in personal property may use simplified affidavit procedures.

SD.notes: South Dakota has no estate tax and is known for favorable trust laws. Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures.

TN.notes: Tennessee eliminated its estate tax in 2016. Small estates under $50,000 in personal property may use simplified affidavit procedures.

TX.notes: Texas has no estate tax. Texas offers several simplified procedures including Muniment of Title (for real property with no debts) and small estate affidavit for estates under $75,000 excluding homestead and exempt property.

UT.notes: Utah has adopted the Uniform Probate Code. Small estates under $100,000 may use simplified procedures.

VT.notes: Vermont has an estate tax with a $5 million threshold. Small estates under $45,000 may use simplified procedures.

VA.notes: Virginia has no estate tax. Small estates under $50,000 in personal property may use a simplified affidavit procedure (Small Estate Act).

WA.notes: Washington State has an estate tax with a $2.193 million threshold for 2024. Small estates under $100,000 may use simplified affidavit procedures.

WV.notes: West Virginia has no estate tax. Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified procedures.

WI.notes: Wisconsin has no estate tax. Small estates under $50,000 may use simplified transfer procedures.

WY.notes: Wyoming has no estate tax and one of the highest small estate thresholds at $200,000. Known for favorable trust and asset protection laws.
